meta.title: Devya — System Flow Map
meta.description: How the Devya apps fit together: who logs in where, what each app does, where data flows.

home.title: How Devya Solutions Works
home.intro: A plain-English map of who does what, which app each team lives in, and how a client goes from booking a call to signing a contract — without any technical jargon.
home.legendCaption: These colours repeat across every diagram, so once you learn them here, the rest scans instantly.
home.bigPictureIntro: Four teams. Nine apps — marketing, booking, sales, quote, tasks, contracts, feedback, mailer, and the admin dashboard. One shared database keeps every screen on the same truth in real time (the mailer is the one standalone tool).
home.journeyTitle: A client journey, end to end
home.journeyIntro: From the very first website visit to a signed contract — one slide, no jargon. The diagram has two lanes: the
home.bookingFlowIntro: Client books a slot → the team accepts or proposes alternatives → confirmation auto-creates a Meeting task with reminders.
home.salesFlowIntro: Reps track leads through stages, log every touch (WhatsApp / call / meeting), schedule meetings straight into the booking calendar, close won leads with a one-click contract prefill, and lose the rest with a reason.
home.feedbackFlowIntro: After a project ships, the Feedback app emails the client a one-tap rating. Happy clients (4–5★) are sent straight to a public platform (Google / Clutch); unhappy ones (1–3★) leave a private comment the team reads first — so public reviews stay strong and problems get caught early.
home.contractFlowIntro: Sales drafts a contract from a template, sends an email with a sign link, and watches the status move from
home.clientSees2a: Lands on the contract page (no login — the email link is the credential). Status flips to
home.clientSees3a: Types their name, draws a signature, ticks "I agree", hits sign. Status flips to
home.clientSees4a: Gets the signed copy back by email (PDF + secure HTML link).
home.relateIntro: One booking can trigger one Meeting task, one quote, and one contract. All of them stay linked so the team can jump between screens without losing context.
home.marketingFlowIntro: Edit in Admin → save → public site refreshes within 60s. No deploys, no redeploys.

marketing.name: Marketing Site
marketing.desc: The public-facing devya.dev — homepage, services, projects, blog.

booking.name: Booking App
booking.desc: Clients pick a free slot. Requests land in our Tasks app as Meeting Requests.

tasks.name: Tasks App
tasks.desc: Where Muhammed & Moaz live every day. Meeting requests appear as a bar at the top.

sales.name: Sales App
sales.desc: Arabic-first pipeline. Reps run leads, log activity, schedule meetings, close deals.

quote.name: Quote App
quote.desc: Clients build a quote from services and see a live invoice, then convert it into a contract.

contracts.name: Contracts App
contracts.desc: Generate a contract from a template, send by email, client signs in browser.

feedback.name: Feedback App
feedback.desc: After delivery, sends a review request. Happy clients go to Google/Clutch; unhappy ones leave private feedback first.

xray.name: X-Ray
xray.desc: Engineering-review consultancy. Clients run a self-assessment and get an interactive report — bilingual, with a paid deep-dive and a free snapshot.

mailer.name: Mailer App
mailer.desc: Internal outbound-email composer that sends branded messages from the company mailbox. Its own login and SMTP — standalone.

admin.name: Admin Dashboard
admin.desc: Edit the marketing site, watch every booking + contract, and manage users, roles & access.

marketing.title: Marketing
marketing.duty2: Edit homepage, services, projects, testimonials, blog

sales.title: Sales / Front desk
sales.duty1: Watch the Meeting Requests bar in the Tasks app
sales.duty2: Accept a slot OR propose 1–10 alternatives
sales.duty4: After meeting, draft a contract from a template

legal.title: Legal / Contracts
legal.duty1: Open Contracts app from the Admin sidebar
legal.duty2: Pick NDA, Project, Consultancy or Retainer template
legal.duty3: Fill client + scope + price, click Send
legal.duty4: Track status from the Admin dashboard

delivery.title: Delivery / Team
delivery.duty1: Confirmed meeting auto-creates a Meeting task
delivery.duty3: Email reminders 8h and 3h before the meeting
delivery.duty4: Mark complete with proof from the Tasks app

bookingToTasks.note: Each confirmed slot auto-creates a "Meeting" task — important by default, urgency follows the date.

tasksToAdmin.note: Every booking + meeting task is also visible in Admin under /bookings.

contractsToAdmin.note: Every contract appears in /contracts with live status (DRAFT → SIGNED) and a signed-PDF download.

quoteToContracts.note: A finished quote converts into a prefilled contract — services, scope and price carry straight over.

wonToFeedback.note: After delivery, a review request goes out; the rating and any private comment land back with the team.

mailerToMailbox.note: Standalone tool with its own login + SMTP — the team composes and sends branded outbound email; it does not touch the shared database.

data.dbBody: Postgres on the Hostinger VPS holds users & roles, bookings, sales leads, contracts, tasks, feedback, and every CMS entity. Every app (except the standalone mailer) reads from this single source of truth, so screens never disagree.
data.imagesBody: Uploads from the Admin dashboard go straight to Cloudflare Images. The marketing site pulls them via

login.title: Devya Flow Map
login.subtitle: This document is confidential. Enter the access password to continue.

credentials.title: Sign-in credentials
credentials.subtitle: For the marketing team and ops admins. Keep these private.
